description_full:
You are given an integer array nums of size n containing only 1 and -1, and an integer k.

You can perform the following operation at most k times:


	
	Choose an index i (0 <= i < n - 1), and multiply both nums[i] and nums[i + 1] by -1.
	


Note that you can choose the same index i more than once in different operations.

Return true if it is possible to make all elements of the array equal after at most k operations, and false otherwise.

 
Example 1:


Input: nums = [1,-1,1,-1,1], k = 3

Output: true

Explanation:

We can make all elements in the array equal in 2 operations as follows:


	Choose index i = 1, and multiply both nums[1] and nums[2] by -1. Now nums = [1,1,-1,-1,1].
	Choose index i = 2, and multiply both nums[2] and nums[3] by -1. Now nums = [1,1,1,1,1].



Example 2:


Input: nums = [-1,-1,-1,1,1,1], k = 5

Output: false

Explanation:

It is not possible to make all array elements equal in at most 5 operations.


 
Constraints:


	1 <= n == nums.length <= 105
	nums[i] is either -1 or 1.
	1 <= k <= n

subtasks.intent: Calculate the minimum operations to transform the array into all 1s.
Calculate the minimum operations to transform the array into all -1s.
Determine if either target state (all 1s or all -1s) is achievable within k operations.
